person.name: Joan de Somery
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Father Sir Roger de Somery, Baron Dudley b. 24 Jun 1255, d. 11 Oct 1291

Mother Agnes d. 23 Nov 1308

Joane de Somery was born circa 1292; Age 30 in 1322.

She married Sir Thomas Botetourt, son of Sir John Botetourt, 1st Lord Botetourt, Admiral of the North Fleet, Constable of St. Briavels Castle, Governor of Framlingham Castle and Maud FitzThomas, before 1318.

They had 1 son (Sir John, 2nd Lord Botetourt) & 1 daughter (Maud, wife of Sir Reynold, 4th Lord Grey of Wilton).

Joane de Somery died circa 12 February 1338.

«b»Family«/b»
Sir Thomas Botetourt b. c 1292, d. c 28 Jul 1322

«b»Children«/b»
1.) Maud Botetourt b. c 1316, d. 14 Sep 1391

2.) Sir John Botetourt, 2nd Lord Botetourt b. bt Aug 1317-Dec 1317, d. 4 Apr 1386

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Somery於大約1292年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾杜德利時，她的父親Roger de Somery III38歲，母親Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel38歲。她於1314年與Thomas Botetourt在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1338年2月12日之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Somery于大约1292年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾杜德利时，她的父亲Roger de Somery III38岁，母亲Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel38岁。她于1314年与Thomas Botetourt在英国英格兰斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1338年2月12日之前。

nameMeanings.name: Somery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Abelino, Adela, Alberto, Alfredo, Ana, Angel, Asuncion, Domingo, Elena, Emiliano, Emilio, Eusebio.
Spanish: possibly a habitational name from La Somera in Cuenca province, named from somero ‘shallow’. This surname is most common in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Somery was born about 1292, in Dudley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Roger de Somery III, was 38 and her mother, Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel, was 38. She married Thomas Botetourt in 1314, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died before 12 February 1338.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Somery est née environ 1292, à Dudley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger de Somery III, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Botetourt en 1314, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée avant 12 février 1338.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Somery um 1292 in Dudley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger de Somery III 38 und ihre Mutter Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel 38. Sie hat 1314 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Botetourt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb vor 12. Februar 1338.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Somery è nata il circa 1292 nel Dudley, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Roger de Somery III, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel, aveva 38 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Botetourt nel 1314, nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì prima del 12 febbraio 1338.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Somery(이)가 대략 1292년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 드들리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Somery III(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1314년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Thomas Botetourt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1338년 2월 12일 이전 사망했다.
